commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Because it enters not into his heart, but into the belly, and goes out into the drain, purging all foods. Cornelius a Lapide Because it entereth not into his heart, i. e, into his soul, and cannot therefore defile it. But goeth into the belly, where the purer portion of the food, being separated, proceeds to the liver and heart; but that which is impure and feculent into the draught, by its going forth, purging, i. e, leaving pure all meats. For in that it, the impure, goeth away, it cleanses and purifies the remainder of the food.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
